medical_history.chief_complaint: Enamel of her teeth was worm-eaten in appearance
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Severe polyhydramnios detected antenatally, poor feeding and failure to thrive at 2 months, diagnosed with impaired renal concentration at 1 year old, hypokalaemia and hypercalciuria at 18 months, diagnosed with amelogenesis imperfecta (AI) and bilateral nephrocalcinosis at 12 years old
medical_history.past_medical_history: Impaired renal concentration, hypokalaemia, hypercalciuria, amelogenesis imperfecta (AI), bilateral nephrocalcinosis
medical_history.family_history: Parents are first cousins, no siblings, no family history of diabetes mellitus or hearing loss

physical_examination.general_examination: Height 145 cm (-1.0SD), normal appearance and intelligence, normal secondary sex characteristics, normal blood pressure
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Extremely thin enamel, rough and extensively cracked surface of permanent teeth, bilateral nephrocalcinosis identified by ultrasonography
